required.description: Category 1: Required - Mandatory compliance unless formally deviated

advisory.description: Category 2: Advisory - Should be followed unless reasoned deviation

1.1.title: C code shall conform to ISO/IEC 9899:2011
1.1.description: All code shall conform to the C11 standard.
1.1.rationale: Ensures portability and defined behavior across compilers.

1.3.title: No undefined or critical unspecified behavior
1.3.description: There shall be no occurrence of undefined or critical unspecified behavior.
1.3.rationale: Prevents unpredictable runtime behavior in safety-critical systems.

2.1.title: No unreachable code
2.1.description: A project shall not contain unreachable code.
2.1.rationale: Dead code increases maintenance burden and may indicate logic errors.

2.2.title: No dead code
2.2.description: There shall be no dead code.
2.2.rationale: Dead code does not affect program operation but increases complexity.

3.1.title: Character sets and encoding
3.1.description: The character sequences /* and // shall not be used within comments.

5.9.title: Internal linkage identifiers unique
5.9.description: Identifiers with internal linkage should be unique.

8.2.title: Function prototypes with named parameters
8.2.description: Function types shall be in prototype form with named parameters.

8.4.title: Compatible declaration visible
8.4.description: A compatible declaration shall be visible when an object/function with external linkage is defined.

8.6.title: Single external definition
8.6.description: An identifier with external linkage shall have exactly one external definition.

8.8.title: Static for internal linkage
8.8.description: The static storage class specifier shall be used in all declarations of objects and functions that have internal linkage.

8.13.title: Pointer to const where possible
8.13.description: A pointer should point to a const-qualified type whenever possible.
8.13.rationale: Documents read-only intent and enables optimizations.

9.1.title: No use before initialization
9.1.description: The value of an object with automatic storage duration shall not be read before it has been set.

10.1.title: Operands of appropriate essential type
10.1.description: Operands shall not be of an inappropriate essential type.

10.3.title: No assignment to narrower type
10.3.description: The value of an expression shall not be assigned to an object with a narrower essential type.

10.4.title: Same essential type category in operations
10.4.description: Both operands shall have the same essential type category.

11.1.title: No conversion between function pointer and other types
11.1.description: Conversions shall not be performed between pointer to function and other types.

11.3.title: No cast between different object pointer types
11.3.description: A cast shall not be performed between a pointer to object type and a pointer to a different object type.
11.3.rationale: Prevents aliasing violations and undefined behavior.

11.6.title: No cast between pointer to void and arithmetic type
11.6.description: A cast shall not be performed between pointer to void and an arithmetic type.

11.8.title: Cast shall not remove const/volatile qualification
11.8.description: A cast shall not remove any const or volatile qualification.

12.1.title: Explicit operator precedence
12.1.description: The precedence of operators within expressions should be made explicit.
12.1.rationale: Improves readability and prevents precedence errors.

12.2.title: Shift range check
12.2.description: The right hand operand of a shift operator shall lie in the range zero to one less than the width.

13.1.title: No persistent side effects in initializers
13.1.description: Initializer lists shall not contain persistent side effects.

13.2.title: Consistent expression evaluation
13.2.description: The value of an expression and its persistent side effects shall be the same under all permitted evaluation orders.

13.5.title: No side effects in right operand of && or ||
13.5.description: The right hand operand of a logical && or || operator shall not contain persistent side effects.
13.5.rationale: Side effects may not occur due to short-circuit evaluation.

14.4.title: Boolean controlling expressions
14.4.description: The controlling expression shall have essentially Boolean type.
14.4.rationale: Clarifies intent and prevents accidental assignments.

15.5.title: Single point of exit
15.5.description: A function should have a single point of exit at the end.
15.5.rationale: Simplifies control flow analysis and resource management.
15.5.notes: Major focus area for this project. See deviation permit PERF-001 for exceptions.

15.6.title: Compound statement for loops and selection
15.6.description: The body of an iteration-statement or a selection-statement shall be a compound-statement.
15.6.rationale: Prevents dangling else and other issues.

15.7.title: Else clause for if-else-if
15.7.description: All if...else if constructs shall be terminated with an else statement.
15.7.rationale: Ensures all cases are handled explicitly.

16.1.title: Well-formed switch statements
16.1.description: All switch statements shall be well-formed.

16.3.title: Unconditional break in switch clause
16.3.description: An unconditional break statement shall terminate every switch-clause.

16.4.title: Default label in switch
16.4.description: Every switch statement shall have a default label.

17.2.title: No recursion
17.2.description: Functions shall not call themselves, either directly or indirectly.
17.2.rationale: Prevents stack overflow in embedded systems.

17.4.title: Return statement in non-void function
17.4.description: All exit paths from a function with non-void return type shall have an explicit return statement.

17.7.title: Use of non-void return value
17.7.description: The value returned by a function having non-void return type shall be used.
17.7.rationale: Unused return values may indicate logic errors.
17.7.notes: Major focus area for this project. Exceptions for (void) cast documented.

17.8.title: Function parameter should not be modified
17.8.description: A function parameter should not be modified.
17.8.rationale: Improves clarity and prevents aliasing issues.

18.1.title: Pointer arithmetic within array bounds
18.1.description: A pointer resulting from arithmetic shall address an element of the same array.

18.3.title: Relational operators on same object pointers
18.3.description: The relational operators shall not be applied to objects of pointer type except where they point into the same object.
18.3.rationale: Comparison of unrelated pointers is undefined.

18.6.title: No address of automatic storage to persistent object
18.6.description: The address of an object with automatic storage shall not be copied to another object that persists after the first object has ceased to exist.

20.7.title: Macro parameter parentheses
20.7.description: Expressions resulting from the expansion of macro parameters shall be enclosed in parentheses.
20.7.rationale: Prevents operator precedence issues with macro arguments.
20.7.notes: Major focus area for this project. Fix: Add parentheses around macro parameters.

21.3.title: No standard library memory management
21.3.description: The memory allocation and deallocation functions of <stdlib.h> shall not be used.
21.3.rationale: Dynamic memory can cause heap fragmentation and is non-deterministic.
21.3.notes: Use static allocation or memory pools instead. See platform/memory_pool.h

21.6.title: No standard library input/output
21.6.description: The Standard Library input/output functions shall not be used.
21.6.rationale: stdio is not suitable for embedded systems.

priority_rules.action: Refactor functions to single exit point
Check or explicitly discard return values

TP-001.title: Third-Party Code
TP-001.description: External library code not under project control
TP-001.justification: Third-party libraries have their own quality assurance processes

PERF-001.title: Performance-Critical Code
PERF-001.description: Timing constraints require specific coding patterns
PERF-001.justification: ISR latency requirements necessitate early returns

HW-001.title: Hardware Access Patterns
HW-001.description: Memory-mapped register access requires specific casts
HW-001.justification: Hardware register access requires integer-to-pointer conversions
